title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels arrived in Boston for the six-day Sail Boston 250 event.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail into Boston Harbor, with ships from over 20 countries participating.
Thousands of spectators gathered across Boston's waterfronts to view the concluding stop of the Sail250 tour.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox draft shortstop Jake Schaffner at No. 20 in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked 75th by MLB.com, played for UNC after transferring from North Dakota State.
Schaffner recorded a .356 average, 50 RBI, and 26 stolen bases during UNC's College World Series run.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston 2026 Grand Parade of Sail featured historic tall ships led by the USS Constitution.
Spectators watched ships from Castle Island to Piers Park, honoring the maritime history of Boston and East Coast pioneers.
The event included global and local vessels like Canada's Bluenose Two, Bermuda's Spirit, and Massachusetts' Schooner Adventure.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello was named the midseason AL 'Cy Yuk' winner by The Athletic for his poor first-half pitching performance.
Bello recorded a 10.35 ERA in eight starts, resulting in his demotion to Triple-A Worcester by the Red Sox.
Despite poor starting stats, Bello excelled in relief with a 0.71 ERA, but was still not recalled to start Saturday.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Red Sox are near a wild-card spot after a seven-game winning streak.
Boston faces a dilemma at the trade deadline: buy for a playoff push or sell assets.
Craig Breslow's job may hinge on his management of the Red Sox's 2026 trade strategy.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden on July 10, 2026, as part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour.
The concert featured a mix of Shakira's hits and new material, including Grammy-winning album tracks from 2024.
Shakira's dynamic performance included various dance styles and highlighted her resilience amid personal life challenges.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: $18.6 million in tax credits will build 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities, including Fall River and Lawrence.
The Housing Development Incentive Program supports projects converting historic buildings and mills into apartments across Massachusetts.
Gov. Healey's administration has awarded HDIP tax credits to 52 projects, creating over 3,400 homes in 16 cities.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, from Townsend, Massachusetts, was arrested by an FBI SWAT team in Albany, New York.
Sawyer is accused of robbing banks in Nashua, NH, and Boonton, NJ, taking $6,000 and $12,000 respectively.
The arrest was part of FBI's Operation Summer Heat 2.0, involving multiple state and federal law enforcement agencies.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, a 41-year-old man from Berlin, NH, was found dead along Route 2 after being missing since July 4.
His body and motorcycle were discovered by a resident in Shelburne, NH, late Thursday night.
Preliminary investigations suggest Grondin crossed into the eastbound lane, hit a post, with no other vehicle involved.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey declared July 11, 2026, 'Noah Kahan Day' in Massachusetts for his musical and mental health contributions.
Noah Kahan became the first artist to headline four sold-out shows at Fenway Park, drawing over 150,000 fans.
Kahan's songs and nonprofit initiatives focus on New England life and mental health awareness, enhancing his regional impact.
